expert_rss.title: Europe’s Expanding Hybrid Threat: Taking a Page from the Proxy Playbook
expert_rss.summary:
On Wednesday, two Jewish men were stabbed in Golders Green, a Jewish area of north London, in an attack police declared a terrorist incident. Authorities are still investigating the motive, but the attack fit the broader pattern of recent antisemitic violence and low-level asymmetric activity that has followed the U.S.-Israeli-led war on Iran, and more […]
The post Europe’s Expanding Hybrid

expert_rss.title: Iraq is Caught up in the U.S.-Iran War
expert_rss.summary:
The U.S.-Iran war is upending Baghdad’s decade-long effort to remain out of the crossfire between Tehran and Washington, as well as its ambition to reintegrate with the Arab world after the many years of isolation during the rule of Saddam Hussein. Iran-aligned militias in Iraq, whose commanders operate autonomously but represent significant segments of the […]
The post Iraq is Caught up in

expert_rss.title: Azerbaijan and Kazakhstan Move to Institutionalize the Middle Corridor
expert_rss.summary: Executive Summary: On April 2, Kazakhstani Prime Minister Olzhas Bektenov announced at a meeting of the Organization of Turkic States (OTS) in Baku that Kazakhstan intends to sign an intergovernmental agreement with Azerbaijan on the Middle Corridor. He emphasized that the initiative has become particularly relevant amid escalating geopolitical tensions and ongoing disruptions to global […]

expert_rss.title: State-Driven Nuclear Expansion is Winning Energy Race
expert_rss.summary:
Executive Summary: The People’s Republic of China (PRC) is on track to have the largest nuclear energy sector in the world by 2030. According to the latest annual blue book published by the China Nuclear Energy Association (CNEA; 中国核能行业协会), by the end of 2025 the country operated 59 commercial reactors totaling more than 62 gigawatts electric […]
The post State-Driven Nuclear Expansion

expert_rss.title: Moscow Trying but Failing to Combat Demographic Collapse of Russian Far East
expert_rss.summary:
Executive Summary: The population of the Russian Far East continues to decline at an accelerating rate, now faster than in any other region of the Russian Federation except the Far North. Russian President Vladimir Putin has frequently pointed to this development as a threat to his country’s national security (URA.RU, June 6, 2023). The number […]
The post Moscow Trying but Failing to Combat

expert_rss.title: Russia Continues Creation of Sovereign National Internet
expert_rss.summary:
Executive Summary: The Russian state is continuing its rapid creation of a sovereign national internet space. It has established new funding and initiatives to block civilian use of virtual private networks (VPNs), expand internet surveillance, and consolidate control over telecommunications providers. The Russian Ministry of Digital Technologies announced plans at the end of March to […]

expert_rss.summary:
Executive Summary: Russian Presidential Press Secretary Dmitry Peskov said on April 3 that Russia will continue its war against Ukraine “until its objectives are achieved” (RIA Novosti, April 3). On April 12, he claimed that Russia only has 17–18 percent of the so-called Donetsk People’s Republic left “to liberate,” after which “very complex” negotiations with […]

expert_rss.title: Han Ethnonationalism in China: From Historical Nostalgia to Transnational Extremism Influences
expert_rss.summary: In the last year, the visibility of Han ethnonationalism in China has been on the rise. Described by scholars as “Han Supremism” or “Imperial Han Nationalism,” this movement includes adherents with varied agendas but is broadly united by the pursuit of a Han-superior social, cultural, and political order in China. The movement should be considered extremist as it promotes an […]

expert_rss.title: Islamic State Translators Push for Rebound in 2026
expert_rss.summary: Official and volunteer translators of Islamic State (IS) propaganda have made attempts to rebound in 2026, after laying the groundwork in the second half of 2025.

expert_rss.title: War Department's $1.5 Trillion Budget Proposal Includes Sizable Nuclear Triad Investment
expert_rss.summary: The War Department aims to modernize the U.S. nuclear triad — including air, land and sea weapons — and the fiscal year 2027 budget proposal includes ample investment to make that happen, said Secretary of War Pete Hegseth during testimony on Capitol Hill.

expert_rss.title: Personal Property Activity Launches Moving Website
expert_rss.summary: The War Department's Personal Property Activity launches its new website, www.ppa.mil, creating a centralized digital hub designed to better support DOW personnel and industry partners as they navigate the moving process.

expert_rss.title: Nevada Guard's Civil Support Team Trains for Radiological, Explosive Threats
expert_rss.summary: The Nevada National Guard's 92nd Civil Support Team conducted its annual "Beta Burn" exercise at the Ormsby House in Carson City, training alongside local and federal partners to respond to simulated radiological and explosive threats.

expert_rss.title: Spacecom Takes Operational Control of Facility in Alabama
expert_rss.summary: U.S. Space Command took control of a facility at Redstone Arsenal, Ala., planting its flag for the first operational element of its new headquarters — the Joint Intelligence Support Element.

expert_rss.title: Reauthorizations Accelerate Tech for War Department
expert_rss.summary: The reauthorization of a couple of small business technology programs opens the gates for the War Department to get cutting-edge technology more quickly into the hands of warfighters.

expert_rss.title: Joint Interagency Task Force 401, Paratroopers Test New Counter-UAS
expert_rss.summary: Paratroopers assigned to the 82nd Airborne Division recently took part in the inaugural training event with the Bumblebee V2 counter-drone system, marking the first operational test of the prototypes purchased by Joint Interagency Task Force 401 earlier this year.

expert_rss.title: National Guard Military Police Battalion Deploys in Support of Operation Epic Fury
expert_rss.summary: Approximately 150 soldiers assigned to the 192nd Military Police Battalion, Connecticut Army National Guard, participated in a deployment ceremony after being selected to deploy on short notice to the Middle East in support of Operation Epic Fury.

expert_rss.title: Hegseth, Caine Say Budget Increases Lethality, Strengthens Defense Industrial Base
expert_rss.summary: The War Department's $1.5 trillion fiscal year 2027 budget request reflects the urgency of the moment, addressing both the deferment of long-standing problems, as well as positioning the joint force for the current and the future fight, Secretary of War Pete Hegseth said during a House Armed Services Committee hearing.

expert_rss.title: Marines Debut New Ground Sensor Systems
expert_rss.summary: Marines showed Tunisian special forces soldiers how the newest generation of ground sensor systems can support security efforts in complex environments during Exercise African Lion 26.

expert_rss.title: Socom Keeps America Safe With Wide Focus
expert_rss.summary: America's Special Operations Command has eyes and ears around the world focused on a variety of hotspots and threats that may put the well-being and safety of the homeland at risk.

expert_rss.title: Trump At A Crossroads For Continuing The War With Iran
expert_rss.summary:
The president is reportedly discussing new options for attacking Iran while facing a legal deadline on continuing the conflict.
The post Trump At A Crossroads For Continuing The War With Iran appeared first on The War Zone.

expert_rss.title: Air Force Wants To Axe Its E-11A BACN Communications Jets
expert_rss.summary:
The Air Force wants space-based systems to take over from the E-11A in providing critical data fusion and communications relay capabilities.
The post Air Force Wants To Axe Its E-11A BACN Communications Jets appeared first on The War Zone.

expert_rss.title: Marine Amphibious Combat Vehicles To Get Missile-Swatting Active Protection Systems
expert_rss.summary:
Active Protection Systems will give Marine ACVs a critical new layer of defense against anti-armor threats that could also help down incoming drones.
The post Marine Amphibious Combat Vehicles To Get Missile-Swatting Active Protection Systems appeared first on The War Zone.

expert_rss.title: B-1B Seen Carrying ARRW Hypersonic Missile For The First Time
expert_rss.summary:
The B-1B will now serve to 2037, and ARRW has emerged out of purgatory in the latest Pentagon budget request.
The post B-1B Seen Carrying ARRW Hypersonic Missile For The First Time appeared first on The War Zone.

expert_rss.title: Israel Now Using Netting To Protect Combat Vehicles Against Scourge Of Hezbollah Drones
expert_rss.summary:
The netting appears as the IDF takes heat for not doing more to defend against FPV drone attacks, although options remain very limited.
The post Israel Now Using Netting To Protect Combat Vehicles Against Scourge Of Hezbollah Drones appeared first on The War Zone.

expert_rss.title: Confidence In CH-53K King Stallion Grows Ahead Of First Operational Deployment
expert_rss.summary:
Going to sea with the 26th Marine Expeditionary Unit is a prelude to the CH-53K hitting full-rate production, planned for FY 2029.
The post Confidence In CH-53K King Stallion Grows Ahead Of First Operational Deployment appeared first on The War Zone.

expert_rss.title: China is America’s Military Equal Now And In Any Future Fight, Marine General Warns
expert_rss.summary:
Faced with the rapidly growing China threat, the U.S. has to start hardening its bases now, said Marine Lt. Gen. Stephen Sklenka.
The post China is America’s Military Equal Now And In Any Future Fight, Marine General Warns appeared first on The War Zone.

expert_rss.title: Marines Realize They Can’t Depend On Army For Ballistic Missile Defense
expert_rss.summary:
The Marines currently rely on already strained Army units to provide defense against incoming ballistic missiles, which could be a big problem in the Pacific.
The post Marines Realize They Can’t Depend On Army For Ballistic Missile Defense appeared first on The War Zone.

expert_rss.title: Marines Offer Glimpse Of New Plan For Its Future Ground Combat Forces
expert_rss.summary:
Ground Combat Element 2040 offers hints into how the Marines expect to fight in a future dominated by AI and autonomous weapons.
The post Marines Offer Glimpse Of New Plan For Its Future Ground Combat Forces appeared first on The War Zone.

expert_rss.title: First USMC MQ-58 Valkyrie CCA Drones To Arrive In 2029
expert_rss.summary:
The MQ-58 with conventional landing gear will mark the beginning of the Marines’ operational Collaborative Combat Aircraft journey, with other types likely to follow.
The post First USMC MQ-58 Valkyrie CCA Drones To Arrive In 2029 appeared first on The War Zone.

expert_rss.title: Some US troops cite benefits of Germany presence as Trump threatens pullback
expert_rss.summary: Germany is the U.S. military’s largest footprint in Europe, with some 35,000 active-duty military personnel, and serves as a key training hub.

wikipedia.notes: Several perpetrators opened fire near the Israeli consulate building, Turkish police neutralized the attackers.
A man opened fire in a supermarket and took hostages before being killed by police. The Security Service of Ukraine said that it was investigating the attack as an act of terrorism.

An individual said to be linked to the terrorist group Harakat Ashab al-Yamin al-Islamia stabbed and injured two Jewish men in an antisemitic attack. The Metropolitan Police declared the incident an act of terrorism.
